sourcesReviewed.title: Applicability of DCB 0129 and DCB 0160 - Step by step guidance
borrowedFor: Scoping whether DCB0129 and DCB0160 apply to a product that influences direct care in England
Making the gate pack fail closed on missing release safety evidence instead of treating safety as optional narrative
sourcesReviewed.notes: Used as current support for the applicability posture. Kept secondary to the local Phase 4 blueprint, which already makes safety evidence mandatory for this exit.

sourcesReviewed.title: Digital clinical safety assurance
borrowedFor: Separating manufacturer-facing DCB0129 and deployment-facing DCB0160 responsibilities
Treating the Clinical Safety Officer, hazard log, and clinical safety case as concrete evidence families rather than soft governance prose
sourcesReviewed.notes: Used to support the conclusion that the missing release-scoped hazard delta is real evidence debt, not a cosmetic doc gap.

sourcesReviewed.title: Trace viewer
borrowedFor: Keeping trace-backed evidence as the primary browser proof surface
Ensuring the board spec asks for screenshots, DOM snapshots, and metadata-backed review rather than static screenshots alone
sourcesReviewed.notes: Used to justify trace-backed board proof and to keep the Playwright verification aligned with official trace-review practice.

sourcesReviewed.title: Visual comparisons
borrowedFor: Documenting that screenshot baselines need a stable environment
Framing the board screenshots as proof of rendering states, not as environment-agnostic pixel guarantees
sourcesReviewed.notes: Used to keep screenshot expectations conservative and environment-specific.

sourcesReviewed.title: Table component
borrowedFor: Table-first evidence scanability
Captions, scope usage, and responsive behaviour that reduces horizontal scrolling on narrow screens
sourcesReviewed.notes: Applied directly to the board layout so every evidence-heavy region has a tabular parity surface.

sourcesReviewed.title: Check answers pattern
borrowedFor: Quiet high-trust summary layout before a final decision statement
Dense but readable two-thirds style content framing for the ribbon and decision copy
sourcesReviewed.notes: Used as a presentation influence only; the board remains a governance surface rather than a transactional confirmation screen.

sourcesReviewed.title: Carbon Design System data table usage
borrowedFor: Dense table-led evidence browsing
Avoiding decorative cards when the user needs to navigate to specific evidence
sourcesReviewed.notes: Used to justify a table-first centre canvas and issue inspector.

sourcesReviewed.title: Navigating the Dashboard
borrowedFor: Quiet hierarchy with saved list-view bias for operational scanability
Dense dashboard framing without celebratory marketing treatment
sourcesReviewed.notes: Used as lightweight visual support for the board structure, especially the list-first evidence view.

sourcesReviewed.title: Triage
borrowedFor: Right-rail issue inspection and ownership-oriented triage framing
Inbox-style treatment of unresolved items rather than burying them in prose
sourcesReviewed.notes: Used to shape the open-issue inspector and make ownership prominent.

sourcesReviewed.title: Display options
borrowedFor: Saved list-vs-board preference thinking
Ordering and grouping controls for dense evidence views
sourcesReviewed.notes: Used as support for the conformance-family selector and filtered evidence tables.

rejectedOrNotAdopted.reason: Rejected for the board UI because the service manual explicitly says the pattern should not be used in transactional services. The exit board needs explicit evidence freshness tables instead of passive review dates.
Grid treatment was rejected because the prompt requires quiet hierarchy, no decorative KPI cards, and table-first scanability.
Expansion-heavy and batch-action-heavy variants were rejected because the board needs direct evidence reading more than secondary row actions.
